title: HRW 180-Page Report: Operation Metro Surge Caused 'Unlawful Killings' and 'Human Rights Crisis' in Minnesota
excerpt: Human Rights Watch published a 180-page report documenting that the federal government's Operation Metro Surge in Minneapolis included two unlawful killings, repeated excessive force, racial profiling, unlawful detentions, and abusive detention conditions — and named those responsible for overseeing the operation.
content:
On June 18, 2026, Human Rights Watch published 'A Manufactured Crisis': Minnesota Communities Terrorized by the Federal Government, a 180-page investigative report comprehensively documenting the human rights impact of the Trump administration's Operation Metro Surge in and around the Twin Cities.

  • The report concluded that the operation included two unlawful killings — Renee Good (January 7) and Alex Pretti (January 24) — as well as repeated instances of excessive force, racial profiling, and unlawful detentions.
  • HRW documented abusive detention conditions and found that the terror caused by enforcement operations forced many residents to miss work, school, and essential health care.
  • The report named specific individuals with command responsibility for overseeing the operation and called for accountability at the highest levels of government.
  • HRW found that DHS had repeatedly issued public statements contradicted by available video evidence, and that federal authorities had blocked state investigators from accessing crime scenes and evidence.

The report described the killings and use-of-force incidents as foreseeable consequences of deploying undertrained officers without adequate crowd-control experience or de-escalation protocols in a densely populated urban environment.
